entities.name: moonshot attempt
entities.context: High-risk objective that forces skill acquisition and perspective shifts even if it fails financially

entities.name: slam dunk success
entities.context: Safe wins that operate within existing capacity, providing validation but minimal growth

entities.name: adaptive stress
entities.context: Mechanism for skill development analogous to biological evolution; structural stress that forces capacity creation

entities.name: growth dividends
entities.context: The skill acquisition and perspective shifts gained from attempting difficult objectives

entities.name: high-order skill acquisition
entities.context: A primary metric for evaluating career paths and business initiatives alongside financial ROI

entities.name: failure with growth
entities.context: Outcome where attempt fails but leaves agent at higher baseline capability

entities.name: success with stagnation
entities.context: Outcome where safe win succeeds but produces no new capacity

relationships.context: Moonshots create adaptive stress by requiring creation of new capacities
Adaptive stress produces growth dividends through forced skill acquisition
Safe wins validate existing capacity without producing growth
Moonshots should be evaluated partly on their potential for skill acquisition
Even failed moonshots leave higher baseline capability than successful slam dunks
Strategic planning should value failure with growth higher than success with stagnation
